sections.paragraph: m. (from the root בָּטַח transp. for טָבַח, طبخ to cook), pl. melons, Num 11:5. To this answers the Arab. بِطّيِخُ transp. for طِبِّيخُ from طبخ to cook, to ripen, like the Gr. πέπων pfebe, melon, from πέπτω: comp. בָּשַׁל. In the Hebrew there is added א prosthetic. From the Arabic word above cited the Spanish budiecas, the French pastêques, are derived.
